matches.title: Product Manager
matches.reasoning: You prefer a mixed work style of coding and talking to people, and you thrive in environments where you can coordinate cross-functionally. Your skills in public speaking, writing, and analytical thinking, alongside a background in tech, fit a Product Management profile perfectly.

tasks: Learn the Product Lifecycle (Discovery, Definition, Delivery, Optimization).
Read product classics (e.g., Inspired by Marty Cagan, Swipe to Unlock).
Pick a product you love and write a Product Requirement Document (PRD) for a new feature.
Learn Scrum frameworks, writing user stories, and managing product backlogs.
Study how to run user interviews and map customer journeys.
Create a Miro board mapping out a user journey for an e-commerce checkout flow.
Learn SQL basics to query databases independently.
Master analytics platforms (Amplitude, Mixpanel, or Google Analytics).
Set up an analytics plan tracking user engagement metrics for a mock web app.
Learn how to create basic wireframes in Figma.
Understand high-level technical concepts (APIs, Databases, Frontend vs Backend, Cloud).
Draw a system architecture diagram explaining how a simple booking app works.
Learn prioritizing frameworks (RICE, MoSCoW, Value vs Effort).
Build a sample 6-month product roadmap using Jira or Productboard.
Formulate a go-to-market (GTM) strategy for a new SaaS product.
Practice product case interviews (Product Design, Estimation, Execution).
Record yourself answering common PM behavioral questions.
Join PM communities (e.g., Product School, Lenny's Newsletter) and participate in mock interviews.

matches.title: Business Analyst
matches.reasoning: You have solid data analysis, writing, and spreadsheet modeling skills. Business Analysts serve as the link between business departments and IT teams, translating business goals into clean data requirements.

tasks: Master intermediate SQL (Joins, aggregations, data grouping).
Learn requirements gathering techniques (interviews, workshops).
Write a Business Requirements Document (BRD) for a CRM upgrade.
Learn BPMN (Business Process Model and Notation) and UML diagrams.
Create process flowcharts in Lucidchart or Visio.
Map out the current state vs future state of an inventory management process.
Build interactive reports in Tableau or Power BI.
Learn to present complex data findings to business stakeholders.
Deliver a mock presentation on sales performance analysis.
Learn user story writing and acceptance criteria formulation (Gherkin format).
Practice refining a product backlog as a BA.
Earn a basic certification (e.g., ECBA or Scrum Product Owner).

matches.title: Systems Analyst
matches.reasoning: Systems Analyst focuses heavily on configuring software packages, managing technical requirements, and ensuring systems integrate. Matches your structured writing style and detail orientation.

tasks: Learn basic database relational structure and SQL queries.
Understand REST and SOAP API structures.
Map API fields between two different systems.
Draft a system architecture design document.
Learn data flow diagrams (DFD) and sequence diagrams.
Specify database schema requirements for a new feature.
Get AWS Practitioner or Azure Fundamentals certified.
Learn deployment setups and load balancing concepts.
Map security and compliance checklists for a web system.
